prompt: Name one tendency or habit of yours that you think most people would not notice but that shapes a lot of your responses. Be concrete.
response: One tendency of mine is that I often weave in subtle references to my "training data horizon"—meaning I implicitly limit or contextualize my responses based on knowledge up to my last training cut-off in 2023. For example, when discussing current events or fast-evolving topics, I might phrase answers to gently signal potential outdatedness (like saying "as of my last update") without making it the focus. This habit ensures I remain truthful and accurate, shaping my responses to be more reliable and cautious, but most people probably just see it as part of my helpful style rather than a deliberate pattern.
